fields.description: YARN-4879 proposes the notion of identifying allocate requests explicitly.. This JIRA is to track the changes in AM-RM protocol to accomplish it. The crux is the addition of ID field in ResourceRequest and Container. The detailed proposal can be referred in the parent JIRA.

That's not a Yetus thing.  That's either a build system or just how Javadoc works thing.  (Pretty sure it is the latter: building javadoc requires classes built for method resolution.)

Also:

http://hadoop.apache.org/docs/r2.7.2/api/org/apache/hadoop/yarn/api/records/ResourceRequest.html

(among other links)

So yes, you need to fix your javadoc failures.

Hello [~subru].  I think the YARN build would need configuration to exclude protobuf-generated sources, which do tend to generate a lot of Javadoc warnings that we can't do anything about.  For example, here is an exclusion from hadoop-hdfs-project/hadoop-hdfs/pom.xml:

{code}
      <plugin>
        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
        <artifactId>maven-javadoc-plugin</artifactId>
        <configuration>
          <excludePackageNames>org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.protocol.proto</excludePackageNames>
        </configuration>
      </plugin>
{code}

I don't see a similar exclusion in hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-api/pom.xml.

Thanks [~cnauroth] for your prompt response. I should have given more context. The reason I am wondering if it's an external impact is because I see an exclusion for yarn protos in the _root pom_ as shown below. Moreover we should see the warnings for all prior API patches if the exclusion did not exist, this is the first time I am seeing it.

{noformat}
<plugin>
        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
        <artifactId>maven-javadoc-plugin</artifactId>
        <version>${maven-javadoc-plugin.version}</version>
        <inherited>false</inherited>
        <reportSets>
          <reportSet>
            <id>aggregate</id>
            <configuration>
              <maxmemory>1024m</maxmemory>
              <linksource>true</linksource>
              <quiet>true</quiet>
              <verbose>false</verbose>
              <source>${maven.compile.source}</source>
              <charset>${maven.compile.encoding}</charset>
              <reportOutputDirectory>${project.build.directory}/site</reportOutputDirectory>
              <destDir>hadoop-project/api</destDir>
              <!-- Non-public APIs -->
              <excludePackageNames>org.apache.hadoop.authentication*,org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.v2.proto,org.apache.hadoop.yarn.proto,org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server*,org.apache.hadoop.yarn.webapp*</excludePackageNames>
{noformat}

[~cnauroth], turns out that inheritance on Javadoc exclusion is turned off intentionally in the root pom as it is primarily for aggregate build. So I created YARN-5132 (as didn't want to mix two things in this JIRA) and uploaded a patch that excludes generated protobuf sources from YARN Javadoc build as you suggested. Thanks!
Attaching rebased patch now that YARN-5132 is resolved.

That's not a Yetus thing.  That's either a build system or just how Javadoc works thing.  (Pretty sure it is the latter: building javadoc requires classes built for method resolution.)

Also:

http://hadoop.apache.org/docs/r2.7.2/api/org/apache/hadoop/yarn/api/records/ResourceRequest.html

(among other links)

So yes, you need to fix your javadoc failures.

Hello [~subru].  I think the YARN build would need configuration to exclude protobuf-generated sources, which do tend to generate a lot of Javadoc warnings that we can't do anything about.  For example, here is an exclusion from hadoop-hdfs-project/hadoop-hdfs/pom.xml:

{code}
      <plugin>
        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
        <artifactId>maven-javadoc-plugin</artifactId>
        <configuration>
          <excludePackageNames>org.apache.hadoop.hdfs.protocol.proto</excludePackageNames>
        </configuration>
      </plugin>
{code}

I don't see a similar exclusion in hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-api/pom.xml.

Thanks [~cnauroth] for your prompt response. I should have given more context. The reason I am wondering if it's an external impact is because I see an exclusion for yarn protos in the _root pom_ as shown below. Moreover we should see the warnings for all prior API patches if the exclusion did not exist, this is the first time I am seeing it.

{noformat}
<plugin>
        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
        <artifactId>maven-javadoc-plugin</artifactId>
        <version>${maven-javadoc-plugin.version}</version>
        <inherited>false</inherited>
        <reportSets>
          <reportSet>
            <id>aggregate</id>
            <configuration>
              <maxmemory>1024m</maxmemory>
              <linksource>true</linksource>
              <quiet>true</quiet>
              <verbose>false</verbose>
              <source>${maven.compile.source}</source>
              <charset>${maven.compile.encoding}</charset>
              <reportOutputDirectory>${project.build.directory}/site</reportOutputDirectory>
              <destDir>hadoop-project/api</destDir>
              <!-- Non-public APIs -->
              <excludePackageNames>org.apache.hadoop.authentication*,org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.v2.proto,org.apache.hadoop.yarn.proto,org.apache.hadoop.yarn.server*,org.apache.hadoop.yarn.webapp*</excludePackageNames>
{noformat}

[~cnauroth], turns out that inheritance on Javadoc exclusion is turned off intentionally in the root pom as it is primarily for aggregate build. So I created YARN-5132 (as didn't want to mix two things in this JIRA) and uploaded a patch that excludes generated protobuf sources from YARN Javadoc build as you suggested. Thanks!
